zh.lifeSummary: 當Johann Friederich Kieffer於1738年出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加宾夕法尼亚州蘭卡斯特時，他的父親Hans Valentin Kieffer47歲，母親Anna Barbara Stober27歲。他於1760年11月25日與Sabina Vollmer在美國賓夕法尼亞州勒班農貝瑟爾 堂史普結婚。他們至少是8個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他於1790年住在美國賓夕法尼亞州約克。他去世在美國賓夕法尼亞州亞當斯利特萊斯托。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Johann Friederich Kieffer于1738年出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加宾夕法尼亚州蘭卡斯特时，他的父亲Hans Valentin Kieffer47岁，母亲Anna Barbara Stober27岁。他于1760年11月25日与Sabina Vollmer在美国宾夕法尼亚州勒班農貝瑟爾 堂史普结婚。他们至少是8个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他于1790年住在美国宾夕法尼亚州約克。他去世在美国宾夕法尼亚州亞當斯利特萊斯托。

nameMeanings.name: Kieffer
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German, Luxembourgish, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of Kiefer . The surname Kieffer is also found in the province of Luxembourg and elsewhere in Wallonia (Belgium).

variants.name: Kiefer
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Franz, Erwin, Heinz, Dieter, Fritz, Alois, Aloys, Claus, Ernst, Florian, Frieda.
German: occupational name for a cooper or the overseer of a wine cellar, from an agent derivative of Middle High German kuofe ‘vat, barrel’ (from Latin cupa).
German: possibly also from an agent derivative of Middle High German kiffen ‘to quarrel’, hence a nickname for a bickerer.

nameMeanings.name: Johann
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: John
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

nameMeanings.name: Friederich
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: from frid ‘peace’ + rīc ‘power’; a royal and imperial name from an early date.

variants.name: Frederick
meanings: From an Old French name of Germanic origin, from fred, frid ‘peace’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’. It was adopted by the Normans and introduced into Britain by them, but did not survive long. Modern use in Britain dates from the 17th century, and it became more frequent in the 18th among followers of the Elector of Hanover, who in 1714 became George I of England. It was reinforced by the vogue for Germanic names in Victorian times.

historicEvents.description: Historical Boundaries: 1749: York, Pennsylvania Colony, British Colonial America 1776: York, Pennsylvania, United States
Thomas Jefferson's American Declaration of Independence endorsed by Congress. Colonies declare independence.
The Declaration of Independence was signed in Philadelphia on July 4, 1776. The liberty bell was first rung here to Celebrate this important document.

historicEvents.title: Movement to Washington D.C.
historicEvents.description: While the growth of the new nation was exponential, the United States didn’t have permanent location to house the Government. The First capital was temporary in New York City but by the second term of George Washington the Capital moved to Philadelphia for the following 10 years. Ultimately during the Presidency of John Adams, the Capital found a permanent home in the District of Columbia.
Historical Boundaries 1810: Adams, Pennsylvania, United States

historicEvents.title: The Second Great Awakening
historicEvents.description: Being a second spiritual and religious awakening, like the First Great Awakening, many Churches began to spring up from other denominations. Many people began to rapidly join the Baptist and Methodist congregations. Many converts to these religions believed that the Awakening was the precursor of a new millennial age.

historicEvents.title: The Oregon Trail
historicEvents.description: Many people started their 2,170-mile West trek to settle the land found by Louis and Clark. They used large-wheeled wagons to pack most of their belongings and were guided by trails that were made by the previous trappers and traders who walked the area. Over time the trail needed annual improvements to make the trip faster and safer. Most of Interstate 80 and 84 cover most of the ground that was the original trail.

en.lifeSummary: When Johann Friederich Kieffer was born in 1738, in Lancaster, Pennsylvania, British Colonial America, his father, Hans Valentin Kieffer, was 47 and his mother, Anna Barbara Stober, was 27. He married Sabina Vollmer on 25 November 1760, in Bethel Township, Lebanon, Pennsylvania, United States. They were the parents of at least 8 sons and 1 daughter. He lived in York, Pennsylvania, United States in 1790. He died in Littlestown, Adams, Pennsylvania, United States.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Johann Friederich Kieffer est né en 1738, à Lancaster, Pennsylvanie, Amérique coloniale britannique, son père, Hans Valentin Kieffer, avait 47 ans et sa mère, Anna Barbara Stober, avait 27 ans. Il a épousé Sabina Vollmer le 25 novembre 1760, à Bethel Township, Lebanon, Pennsylvanie, États-Unis. Ils ont eu au moins 8 fils et 1 fille. Il a vécu à York, Pennsylvanie, États-Unis en 1790. Il est décédé à Littlestown, Adams, Pennsylvanie, États-Unis.

de.lifeSummary: Als Johann Friederich Kieffer 1738 in Lancaster, Pennsylvania, British Colonial America geboren wurde, war sein Vater Hans Valentin Kieffer 47 und seine Mutter Anna Barbara Stober 27. Er hat am 25. November 1760 in Bethel Township, Lebanon, Pennsylvania, Vereinigte Staaten Sabina Vollmer geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 8 Söhne und 1 Tochter. 1790 hat er in York, Pennsylvania, Vereinigte Staaten gelebt. Er starb in Littlestown, Adams, Pennsylvania, Vereinigte Staaten.

ja.lifeSummary: Johann Friederich Kiefferが1738年にイギリス植民地アメリカペンシルベニア州ランカスターで生まれた時，父Hans Valentin Kiefferは47歳，母Anna Barbara Stoberは27歳であった。1760年11月25日にアメリカ合衆国ペンシルベニア州レバノンベスエル トウンシュイップでSabina Vollmerと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも8人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1790年にはアメリカ合衆国ペンシルベニア州ヨークに住んでいた。彼はアメリカ合衆国ペンシルベニア州アダムズリットレストウンで死去した。

ko.Bethel, Lebanon, Pennsylvania, United St: 미국 펜실베이니아 주 레바넌 베테 타운시프
ko.Lancaster, Pennsylvania, British Colonia: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 펜실베이니아 주 랭커스터
ko.lifeSummary: Johann Friederich Kieffer(이)가 1738년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 펜실베이니아 주 랭커스터에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Hans Valentin Kieffer(은)는 47세였고, 그의 어머니 Anna Barbara Stober(은)는 27세였다. 그는 1760년 11월 25일 미국 펜실베이니아 주 레바넌 베테 타운시프에서 Sabina Vollmer(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여덟 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 1790년도에 미국 펜실베이니아 주 요르크에서 살았다. 그는 미국 펜실베이니아 주 애댐스 리틀레스타운에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Johann Friederich Kieffer nasceu em 1738, em Lancaster, Pennsylvania, British Colonial America, seu pai, Hans Valentin Kieffer, tinha 47 anos e sua mãe, Anna Barbara Stober, tinha 27 anos. Ele casou-se com Sabina Vollmer em 25 de novembro de 1760, em Bethel Township, Lebanon, Pensilvânia, Estados Unidos da América. Eles tiveram pelo menos 8 filhos e 1 filha. Ele viveu em York, Pensilvânia, Estados Unidos da América em 1790. Ele faleceu em Littlestown, Adams, Pensilvânia, Estados Unidos da América.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Johann Friederich Kieffer родился в 1738 г. в местности ланкастир, Пенсильвания, бритиш колоняль амирика, его отцу по имени Hans Valentin Kieffer было 47 лет, а матери, которую звали Anna Barbara Stober, 27. Он женился на женщине по имени Sabina Vollmer 25 ноября 1760 г. в местности бетел тоуншип, лебанон, Пенсильвания, Соединенные штаты. У них было по крайней мере 8 сыновей и 1 дочь. Он жил в местности ерк, Пенсильвания, Соединенные штаты в 1790 году. Он умер в местности литлестоун, адамс, Пенсильвания, Соединенные штаты.

es.Bethel, Lebanon, Pennsylvania, United St: Bethel Township, Lebanon, Pensilvania, Estados Unidos
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Johann Friederich Kieffer nació en 1738, en Lancaster, Pensilvania, British Colonial America, su papá, Hans Valentin Kieffer, tenía 47 años y su mamá, Anna Barbara Stober, tenía 27 años. Se casó con Sabina Vollmer el 25 de noviembre de 1760, en Bethel Township, Lebanon, Pensilvania, Estados Unidos. Fueron padres de por lo menos 8 hijos y 1 hija. Vivió en York, Pensilvania, Estados Unidos en 1790. Murió en Littlestown, Adams, Pensilvania, Estados Unidos.
